steps.title: Expand to create an identity
steps.content:
For infinitely many solutions, the equation must be an identity (both sides identical for all $x$). Expand both sides:

Left: $18(x+5) = 18x + 90$

Right: $2(x+c) + 16x = 2x + 2c + 16x = 18x + 2c$

steps.title: Set constant terms equal
steps.content:
For the equation to be an identity:
$$18x + 90 = 18x + 2c$$

The coefficients of $x$ are already identical, so the constant terms must match: $90 = 2c$, giving $c = 45$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
